title: TypeScript Migrations in Prisma Next
description: In Prisma Next, migrations are TypeScript files you can read, edit, and re-run with confidence. Every step is verified before it runs and again after, and every failure points directly at the operation that caused it.

title: MongoDB Without Compromise
description: Prisma Next brings a MongoDB-native experience to TypeScript with type-safe queries, real migrations, polymorphic models, embedded collections, and typed aggregation pipelines.

title: Announcing Query Insights For Prisma Postgres
description: Query Insights gives you built-in visibility into how your database queries behave in production. It is included in Prisma Postgres and works out of the box without any setup.

title: Agentic Engineering: How Prisma Builds with AI
description: In three months, Prisma fundamentally changed how it builds software by rethinking the relationship between engineers and AI agents. Here's what we learned building Prisma Next with agentic engineering.

title: Rethinking Database Migrations
description: Database migrations are brittle and they break when you're most vulnerable; when you're deploying to production. Prisma Next migrations make them explicit, verifiable and safe to retry.

title: Sunsetting Prisma Optimize
description: Prisma Optimize is being sunset as we move query observability directly into Prisma Postgres through Query Insights.

title: Prisma Next Roadmap
description: Have you been wondering when you can start using Prisma Next in your app? Take a look at our roadmap to find out.

title: Rebuilding the Prisma Docs: A New Framework, Design, and Structure
description: Learn how the Prisma team rebuilt their docs from scratch using Fumadocs, improved search with Mixedbread, and launched without disrupting day-to-day work.

title: The Next Evolution of Prisma ORM
description: A look at Prisma Next, a ground-up rethink of Prisma that improves queries, extensibility, migrations, and AI-friendly workflows while keeping Prisma’s schema-first approach.

title: Prisma ORM v7.4: Query Caching & Performance Boost
description: Prisma ORM v7.4 introduces query caching, partial indexes, and BigInt precision fixes—delivering significant performance improvements under load.

title: Prisma 7 Explained: Performance, Architecture, and AMA Answers
description: Prisma 7 introduces major architectural changes. We answer community AMA questions on performance, database support, the new client generator, and what’s next.

title: Prisma ORM 7.3.0: Faster Query Compilation, Safer BigInt JSON, Better Adapters
description: Prisma ORM 7.3.0 adds a new compilerBuild option (fast or small), speeds up raw queries by bypassing compilation, improves MSSQL and better-sqlite3 stability, reverts mapped enum behavior, and preserves BigInt precision in relationJoins JSON aggregation.

title: Prisma ORM 7.2.0 Released: CLI Improvements, Bug Fixes & Better Configs
description: Prisma ORM 7.2.0 is out with key improvements: the return of the --url flag, smarter prisma init configs for Node and Bun, better CLI errors, and fixes for @db.Money. Upgrade today.

title: Understanding Prisma 7 Performance Benchmarks
description: We reviewed community benchmarks comparing Prisma 6 and Prisma 7. Here’s what they measure, where regressions exist, and how we’re fixing them.

title: Prisma 7 Release: Rust-Free, Faster, and More Compatible
description: Prisma ORM 7 delivers major performance gains, a Rust-free client, and a streamlined developer experience, plus deeper Prisma Postgres integration.

title: Announcing Prisma 6.19.0
description: Prisma 6.19 release includes pooled Postgres connections and VS Code extension improvements

title: How Deno and Prisma Partnered to Power Per-Branch Databases
description: Deno Deploy integrates Prisma Postgres for instant per-branch databases, enabling seamless full-stack deployment and modern developer workflows.

title: Prisma ORM 6.18.0, Postgres Database Metrics and Config Modernization
description: Catch up on the lates 6.18.0 release for Prisma ORM and Prisma Postgres.

title: The ORM Convergence: Convergence through experience
description: Why Drizzle's relational API v2 mirrors Prisma: production database problems at scale lead to similar patterns. A look at ORM convergence.

title: ORM v6.17.0, New Usage Metrics & Direct Connections in GA for Prisma Postgres
description: Prisma ORM v6.17.0 is out with fixes and improvements. Connecting to Prisma Postgres via your favorite ORM/DB tool is now GA. Read the full update.

title: Introducing Instant App Deployments in Partnership with Vercel
description: Instant App Deployment with Prisma Postgres and Vercel makes going from generated code to production effortless. Provision a Postgres database, deploy globally, and let users claim full ownership with a single API call. Perfect for AI coding platforms, no-code tools, and modern app builders.

title: Rust-free Prisma ORM is Ready for Production, ORM v16.6.0 & More
description: The future of Prisma ORM is here: The Rust-free version of Prisma ORM and the ESM-first prisma-client generator are both Generally Available in v6.16.0.

title: Why Prisma ORM Checks Types Faster Than Drizzle
description: Discover why Prisma ORM outperforms Drizzle ORM in TypeScript type checking. Our benchmarks show Prisma checks queries 72% faster on average, thanks to code generation that keeps editors snappy and CI runs smooth.

title: ORM 6.15.0, AI Safety Guardrails for Destructive Commands & More
description: Protect your DB from AI mishaps with Prisma ORM v6.15.0—new safety guardrails, ESM-first client updates & better Postgres tooling.

title: Prisma ORM v6.14.0, Relationships for SQL Views, More Robust Management API & More
description: We just released Prisma ORM v6.14.0 with the ability to define relations across views, a robust API for programmatically provisioning Prisma Postgres & more.

title: How MasterBoard Simplified CI/CD Testing with Prisma Postgres
description: MasterBoard replaced AWS with Prisma Postgres to simplify infrastructure and streamline testing. Discover how they spin up fresh databases for every PR, test with real data, and ship faster with less setup, more speed, and top-tier support from Prisma.

title: ORM 6.13.0, CI/CD Workflows & pgvector for Prisma Postgres
description: Prisma ORM v6.13.0 introduces Prisma Config & multi-schema support in GA, pgvector for Prisma Postgres, a new Management API for CI/CD, SQL view enhancements, and a redesigned Prisma Console UI.

title: Why SiteGPT Chose Prisma Postgres for Scalable AI-Powered Chatbots
description: Learn why SiteGPT migrated from their previous database to Prisma Postgres. Discover how transparent pricing, zero server management, and seamless ORM integration provided the simplicity and confidence they needed to build and scale.

title: Prisma ORM without Rust: Latest Performance Benchmarks
ORM 6.12.0: ESM-Compatible Generator in Preview & New Options for Prisma Config
description: Check out the latest Prisma release with our new ESM-compatible generator and New Options for Prisma Config!

title: How Co.dev Uses Prisma Postgres to Power AI-Driven App Development for Non-Developers
description: Discover why Co.dev chose Prisma Postgres: simple API & MCP integration, explicit schema support, and one-shot authentication in its AI-driven app creation flow.

title: ORM v6.11.0, Embedded Prisma Studio, Rust-free ORM for MySQL in Preview & More
description: What’s new: Prisma ORM without Rust in Preview for MySQL & Neon, embedded Prisma Studio for your React apps, new region for Prisma Postgres & more.

title: Optimizing Postgres for Global Apps
How Bucket Uses Prisma ORM to Make Shipping Feature Releases Faster
description: Learn how Bucket uses Prisma

title: ORM 6.10.0, New Features for Prisma Postgres, Remote MCP & More
description: This week comes packed with major improvements for Prisma Postgres & Prisma ORM. Read this article to learn more!

title: Prisma 6.9.0: Rust-Free ORM in Preview, Connect To Prisma Postgres With Any Tool, & More
description: Explore Prisma 6.9.0: Connect any tool to Prisma Postgres (Drizzle, Kysely, etc.), try the new Rust-free Prisma ORM for PostgreSQL & SQLite, run local Prisma Postgres with persistent data, and manage databases via a new VS Code UI.

title: Vibe Coding an E-commerce App with Prisma MCP and Next.js
description: Learn how we used Prisma MCP and Next.js to rapidly build an e-commerce app through vibe coding.

title: Why Prisma ORM Generates Code into Node Modules & Why It’ll Change
description: Prisma has historically generated client code into node_modules, but this is changing in the future.

title: Operations-Based Billing: A Simpler Pricing Model for Modern Databases
description: Serverless pricing for databases is notoriously difficult to grok and predict accurately. When thinking of how to solve this pricing dilemma for Prisma Postgres, we wanted to make things easy. Our operations-based pricing model simplifies pricing and removes the need to think about resource sizes or abstract compute time metrics.

title: Prisma 6.8.0: VS Code Agent Mode, Local Prisma Postgres, Native Deno Support
description: With Prisma 6.8.0, spin up a local Postgres instance in seconds, create databases with VS Code Agent Mode, query docs via GitHub Copilot, and run Prisma ORM natively on Deno.

title: Why the Prisma CLI Asks for Your Feedback
description: To capture the voice of our community we’ve started prompting for feedback directly in the CLI—an unconventional choice that deserves a short explanation.

title: About MCP Servers & How We Built One for Prisma
Try the New "Rust-free" Version of Prisma ORM (Early Access)
Working With Data in Large Teams
description: Scale your data workflows without slowing down your team. This guide helps engineering leads adopt CI for database changes, define clear ownership, and balance velocity with safety using proven patterns like expand-contract and schema validation.

title: Announcing: Prisma Postgres Integration for Vercel Marketplace
description: We’re thrilled to announce that you can now create and manage Prisma Postgres instances without leaving your Vercel Dashboard.

title: Make Your Applications Fast Globally
description: Speed up edge and serverless apps by fixing database latency, cold starts, and slow queries.

title: Securely Access Prisma Postgres from the Frontend (Early Access)
description: Define security rules for Prisma Postgres in TypeScript and securely talk to your database directly from your React, Vue, Svelte, or any other frontend apps.

title: Vibe Coding with Limits — How to Build Apps in the Age of AI
description: Discover how developers can use AI coding tools to build robust, maintainable software without sacrificing quality.

title: Announcing Prisma’s MCP Server: Vibe Code with Prisma Postgres
description: With Prisma’s MCP server, Cursor, Windsurf and other AI tools, can now provision and manage Postgres databases for your apps..

title: Prisma ORM 6.6.0: ESM Support, D1 Migrations & Prisma MCP Server
description: The v6.6.0 Prisma ORM release is packed with exciting features: ESM support, migrations on Cloudflare D1, an MCP server to manage DBs & a lot more.

title: Rust to TypeScript Update: Boosting Prisma ORM Performance
description: A blog post showing how the new Query Compiler project, where the Prisma query engine is being re-written from Rust to TypeScript, is improving performance.

title: Prisma Schema Language: The Best Way to Define Your Data
description: An article discussing the Prisma Schema Language and comparing it to TypeScript-based schemas.

title: How GreatFrontEnd Supercharged Development with Prisma ORM
description: Discover how GreatFrontEnd revolutionized their database operations by integrating Prisma ORM—achieving type safety, streamlined schema management, and a more efficient development pipeline.

title: Helping Founders Scale Faster and Build Smarter
Announcing: Instant Prisma Postgres for AI Coding Agents
Prisma Postgres: Now in Your Favorite Environment
description: Integrate Prisma Postgres in your Netlify site in less 5 minutes or quickly try it out without leaving your browser using the official Project IDX template.

title: Cloudflare, Unikernels & Bare Metal: Life of a Prisma Postgres Query
description: Take a look under the hood of the most innovative PostgreSQL database on the market and learn how a query travels through Cloudflare, unikernels and bare metal.

title: Prisma Postgres: The Future of Serverless Databases
description: Prisma Postgres is finally ready for production! Built on unikernels, it enables a unique set of benefits like no cold starts, connection pooling, edge caching, & more!

title: Prisma ORM Architecture Shift: Why We Moved from Rust to TypeScript
description: Why is Prisma ORM switching from Rust to TypeScript? Read the full architectural rationale and what the new Query Compiler means for your app's speed

title: We Made Prisma Studio Even Better
description: Prisma Studio has just received some great new UX/DX upgrades and is now available in the Prisma Console. Read to find out how you can be more productive in Prisma Studio!

title: Database vs Application: Demystifying JOIN Strategies
description: Joining data from multiple tables is a complicated topic. There are two main strategies: database-level and application-level joins. Prisma ORM offers both options. In this article you’ll learn the tradeoffs between the two so you can pick the best strategy.

title: Top 5 Myths about Prisma ORM
description: Discover the truth behind five common misconceptions about Prisma ORM. In this article, we debunk the myths, explore their origins, and separate fact from fiction.

title: Advanced Database Schema Management with Atlas & Prisma ORM
description: Atlas is a powerful data modeling and migrations tool enabling advanced DB schema management workflows, like CI/CD, schema monitoring, versioning, and more.

title: Studio for Prisma Postgres: View and Edit Your Data Online
description: Prisma Studio is now available in the Platform Console for Prisma Postgres databases. It’s the easiest way to explore and manipulate the data in your Prisma Postgres instance.

title: Prisma ORM Manifesto: Clarity and Collaboration
description: Refocusing Prisma on what matters most: clear governance, better issue management, timely feature releases, and community collaboration

title: Prisma 6: Better Performance, More Flexibility & Type-Safe SQL
description: Today, we are releasing Prisma v6! Since the last major version, we have been hard at work incorporating user feedback, making Prisma ORM faster and more flexible, and adding amazing features like type-safe raw SQL queries.

title: Prisma Postgres is completely free during Early Access
description: We decided to make Prisma Postgres free to use during Early Access so everyone can try it

title: Prisma Postgres®: Building a Modern PostgreSQL Service Using Unikernels & MicroVMs
description: At Prisma, we believe that deploying a database should be as simple as adding a new page in Notion. Today, we are excited to share the first milestone towards this vision: Prisma Postgres® gives developers an always-on database with pay-as-you-go pricing, thanks to our unique architecture design.

title: On-Demand Cache Invalidation Now Available in Prisma Accelerate
description: Boost app performance with precise control using Prisma's on-demand cache invalidation.

title: Why Use Prisma to Build Your Data Layer in 2024?
description: Discover how Prisma's powerful tools help you build a scalable, secure, and high-performing data layer. From the popular TypeScript and Node.js ORM to advanced features like connection pooling, caching, and query optimization, Prisma equips you to scale your app to millions of users.

title: Announcing TypedSQL: Make your raw SQL queries type-safe with Prisma ORM
description: Prisma ORM now supports the ability to write raw sql queries and have the inputs and outputs be fully type-safe! Get the benefit of a high-level API with the power of raw SQL.

title: Enhance your database connections with Prisma Accelerate's auto-scaling
description: Prisma Accelerate’s new auto-scaling feature improves connection pooling, ensuring efficient and scalable database management.

title: How Prisma ORM Became the Most Downloaded ORM for Node.js
description: Reflect on three years of Prisma ORM development with this article showing how Prisma ORM became the most downloaded ORM for Node.js

title: Prisma products are now available for purchase on AWS Marketplace
description: AWS Marketplace customers can purchase Prisma products through AWS Marketplace.

title: Performance Benchmarks: Comparing Query Latency across TypeScript ORMs & Databases
description: Which ORM is the fastest? We have created open-source performance benchmarks to compare query latencies for Prisma ORM, TypeORM and Drizzle ORM.

title: How Solin Uses Prisma Accelerate to Serve 2.5M Database Queries per
description: Learn how Prisma Accelerate has contributed to Solin's success by enhancing performance and reliability with its scalable connection pool and global database cache.

title: Prisma's Cloud Connectivity Report 2024
description: The Prisma Cloud Connectivity Report 2024 analyzes AWS and Cloudflare latency, highlighting regions with the fastest and slowest connectivity.

title: Prisma completes HIPAA, GDPR, ISO27001, SOC2 compliance requirements.
description: Prisma completes the compliance requirements for GDPR, HIPAA, ISO27001 and SOC2-TypeII certifications

title: Prisma ORM 5.16.0 allows global or local omitted fields in Prisma Client
description: Prisma ORM v5.16.0 allows you to omit fields globally or per-query. This blog post overviews the change and how to omit fields in your Prisma Client queries.

title: Introducing Prisma Nuxt: Simplifying Data Management for Nuxt Apps
description: Use Prisma ORM in your Nuxt.js app easily with the Prisma Nuxt module for type-safe queries and database management.

title: Organize your Prisma Schema into multiple files with Prisma ORM version 5.15.0
description: With Prisma ORM 5.15.0 you can now use multiple Prisma Schema files instead of just one. Learn how to enable this Preview feature and check out a real-world example.

title: Handle high load on Black Friday with Prisma Accelerate
description: Ensure stability and performance during high traffic periods with Prisma Accelerate's connection pooling

title: Join the Prisma Insider Program and Shape the Future of our Products
Cache your database queries with Prisma Accelerate
description: Achieve faster performance and cost savings with Prisma Accelerate's easy per-query caching, eliminating the need for maintaining caching infrastructure. Experience faster queries, without the hassle!

title: Bringing Prisma ORM to React Native and Expo
description: Prisma ORM now provides Early Access support for React Native and Expo. The integration introduces reactive queries, using React hooks to auto-update the UI when the underlying data changes.

title: Introducing Prisma Optimize in Early Access
description: Explore insights into your database operations to diagnose performance problems and enhance your application's performance and user experience.

title: Explanation of Prisma's Build, Fortify, Grow (BFG) Framework
description: An overview of how Prisma products interoperate at each stage and aid in enhancing data-driven application development.

title: Increase Database Security With Static IP Support in Prisma Accelerate
description: Prisma Accelerate now supports Static IPs, ensuring secure database connections with predictable IPs for controlled access.

title: Build Applications at the Edge with Prisma ORM & Cloudflare D1 (Preview)1
description: Prisma ORM now supports Cloudflare D1 databases. Read this article to learn how to query D1 from a Cloudflare Worker.

title: Prisma ORM Support for Edge Functions is Now in Preview
description: We're thrilled to share that you can now access your database with Prisma ORM from Vercel Edge Functions and Cloudflare Workers.

title: Introducing Platform Environments
description: Introducing Platform Environments & Early Access Prisma Data Platform integration to Prisma CLI.

title: Prisma ORM Now Lets You Choose the Best Join Strategy (Preview)
description: Choose between DB-level and application-level joins to pick the most performant approach for your relation queries.

title: Prisma | A Business Case for Extended ESOP Exercise Windows
description: Explore extending ESOPs to 10 years, considering fairness, economics, benefits, challenges with Prisma's example, and startup compensation.

title: Data DX: The name for Prisma’s developer experience philosophy
description: Explore the evolution of Data DX at Prisma, from its inception before naming to the launch of a new category.

title: We Transitioned Prisma Accelerate to IPv6 Without Anyone Noticing
description: In July 2023, AWS announced the decision to begin charging for IPv4 addresses beginning in February 2024. This move had a major impact on Prisma Accelerate for reasons we’ll get into, prompting us to go all-in on IPv6. Join us for a deep dive into how we approached our IPv6 migration, lessons learned, and the outcome for users of Prisma Accelerate.

title: Formbricks' success story: Enhancing scalability with Prisma Accelerate
description: Learn how Formbricks tackled performance bottlenecks and enhanced server performance and user experience with Prisma Accelerate.

title: Key takeaways from the Discover Data DX virtual event
description: Highlights from the Discover Data DX virtual event hosted by Prisma on December 7th, 2023, where industry leaders discussed the significance and principles of Data DX.

title: Prisma Accelerate now in General Availability
description: Supercharge your applications with Prisma Accelerate's scalable connection pooling and global edge caching. Plus, enjoy our exclusive launch offer.

title: Support for Serverless Database Drivers in Prisma ORM Is Now in Preview
description: Prisma support for JavaScript-based serverless database drivers is now in Preview, enabling you to connect to Neon and PlanetScale via HTTP and WebSockets.

title: Data DX Manifesto: a new era in data-driven app development
description: Prisma introduces Data DX (Data Developer Experience) as a fresh approach for effortlessly building data-driven applications.

title: SQLite on the Edge: Prisma Support for Turso is in Early Access
description: Prisma support for Turso is now in Early Access, enabling you to bring SQLite closer to your users. Try it out!

title: Introducing the Read Replicas Extension for Prisma Client
description: Distribute read traffic across replicas using the new read replicas extension for Prisma Client.

title: Accelerate in Preview: Global Database Cache & Scalable Connection Pool
description: Accelerate is going into Preview! Learn how to enable high-speed, scalable applications with a global cache and connection pooler.

title: Developer Experience Redefined: Prisma & Cloudflare Lead the Way to Data DX
description: Prisma introduces Data DX and announces a partnership with Cloudflare

title: Database Caching: A Double-Edged Sword? Examining the Pros and Cons
description: Discover the advantages and hurdles of caching database query results. Learn how caching enhances performance, scalability, and resource utilization, while also delving into the associated challenges.

title: Prisma 5: Faster by Default
description: Prisma 5.0.0 introduces new changes that make it significantly faster. These changes especially improve the experience using Prisma in serverless environments.

title: Prisma Client Extensions Are Now Production Ready (4.16.0)
description: Make Prisma Client do even more with Client extensions, now Generally Available. Extend your client, models, queries, and results to tailor Prisma Client to your use case.

title: Building on an Established Community: Prisma Now on Discord
description: Prisma is excited to expand the established Prisma Community Discord and further collaborate with the Prisma community.

title: Overcoming Database Challenges in Serverless and Edge Applications
description: Learn best practices around deploying stateful apps in traditionally stateless environments.

title: How Prisma & Serverless Fit Together
description: Learn about how Prisma views the evolution of deployment types, serverless and the edge, and the problems we want to solve in the space.

title: Best Practices To Speed Up Your Serverless Applications
description: Learn about some best practices to speed up your serverless applications using Prisma.

title: How We Sped Up Serverless Cold Starts with Prisma by 9x
description: Learn about the Prisma's goals, the hurdles we are overcoming, and how you can help.

title: Adding Database Access to your SvelteKit App with Prisma
description: Learn how you can interact with a database from a SvelteKit application using Prisma.

title: Building a REST API with NestJS and Prisma: Authentication
description: In this tutorial, you will learn how to implement JWT authentication with NestJS, Prisma and PostgreSQL. You will also learn about salting passwords, security best practises and how to integrate with Swagger.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Testing with Prisma: CI Pipelines
description: Learn how to set up a CI pipeline to automatically run tests against your application that uses Prisma.

title: Building a REST API with NestJS and Prisma: Handling Relational Data
description: In this tutorial, you will learn how to handle relational data in a REST API built with NestJS, Prisma and PostgreSQL. You will create a new User model and learn how to model relations in the data layer and API layer.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Testing with Prisma: End-To-End Testing
description: Learn all about end-to-end testing, how to set up a testing environment and how to write tests using Playwright and Prisma.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Testing with Prisma: Integration Testing
description: Learn about how to plan, set up and write integration tests for your API.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Testing with Prisma: Unit Testing
description: Learn about what unit testing is and how to approach it in an application using Prisma Client.

title: Restructuring Prisma
description: Restructuring Prisma

title: Prisma | Introducing Accelerate in Early Access
description: Query up to 1000x faster on any Prisma-supported database with our new distributed cache and scalable connection pool for Serverless apps.

title: Prisma Is Expanding Its Executive Team
description: Announcement about new COO joining Prisma

title: What's new in Prisma? (Q4/22)
description: Here’s all you need to know about the Prisma ecosystem and community from August to December 2022.

title: The Ultimate Guide to Testing with Prisma: Mocking Prisma Client
description: Learn about mocking and spying, why they are useful, and how they can be done with Prisma Client.

title: Prisma Playground: An Interactive Learning Experience for Prisma
description: Explore the Prisma Client API to send database queries and Prisma Migrate worklfows to evolve your database schema in an interactive environment.

title: Prisma Client Just Became a Lot More Flexible: Prisma Client Extensions (Preview)
description: Learn how Prisma Client extensions (Preview) enable a variety of new use cases for the Prisma ORM.

title: Building a REST API with NestJS and Prisma: Error Handling
description: In this tutorial, you will implement error handling in a NestJS application. You will learn two ways to handle errors: directly in your application code and by creating an exception filter.

title: How TypeScript 4.9 `satisfies` Your Prisma Workflows
description: Learn how TypeScript 4.9''s new `satisfies` operator can help you write type-safe code with Prisma

title: Try Prisma: The Fastest Way to Explore Prisma Examples
description: Announcing: Try Prisma. A CLI utility that allows you to easily set up and try out an example projects using Prisma.

title: Database Metrics with Prisma, Prometheus & Grafana
description: This tutorial will help you to get started with Prisma's metrics feature. Learn how to integrate metrics into a web server using Prometheus and Grafana.

title: Improving query performance with database indexes using Prisma: Hash indexes
description: Learn how you can optimize a slow database query in your application with a Hash index using Prisma

title: Improving query performance with database indexes using Prisma: B-tree indexes
description: Learn how you can optimize a slow database query in your application with a B-Tree index using Prisma

title: Improving query performance with database indexes using Prisma: Introduction
description: Learn the fundamentals of database indexes: what they are, the problem they solve, and their benefit and cost

title: Monitor Your Server with Tracing Using OpenTelemetry & Prisma
description: This tutorial will help you to get started with Prisma's tracing feature and OpenTelemetry in Node.js. Learn how to integrate tracing and OpenTelemetry into a web server built with Express and Prisma.

title: End-To-End Type-Safety with GraphQL, Prisma & React: Codegen & Deployment
description: Learn how to build a fully type-safe application with GraphQL, Prisma, and React. This article walks you through setting up code generation to allow you to keep your TypeScript types in sync across your frontend and API. You will also deploy your completed project.

title: End-To-End Type-Safety with GraphQL, Prisma & React: API Prep
description: Learn how to build a fully type-safe application with GraphQL, Prisma, and React. This article walks you through setting up a TypeScript project, a PostgreSQL database, and Prisma.

title: End-To-End Type-Safety with GraphQL, Prisma & React: GraphQL API
description: Learn how to build a fully type-safe application with GraphQL, Prisma, and React. This article walks you through building a type-safe GraphQL API

title: End-To-End Type-Safety with GraphQL, Prisma & React: Frontend
description: Learn how to build a fully type-safe application with GraphQL, Prisma, and React. This article walks you through building a type-safe React app that accesses a GraphQL API.

title: Prisma Support for Tracing and Metrics Is Now in Preview
description: We are excited to launch two new features in Preview: Tracing and Metrics. Try them out and let us know what you think!

title: What's new in Prisma? (Q2/22)
description: Learn about everything that has happened in the Prisma ecosystem and community from April to July 2022.

title: Database access on the Edge with Next.js, Vercel & Prisma Accelerate
description: Learn how you can query databases in Edge environments using Prisma and the Prisma Accelerate.

title: Prisma announces the Prisma FOSS Fund to support free and open source software teams.
description: Prisma has started a fund to support independent free and open source software (FOSS) teams. Each month, we will donate $5,000 to a selected project to maintain its work and continue its development.

title: Learn how to add Input Validation to a REST API with NestJS and Prisma
description: Learn how to build a backend REST API with NestJS, Prisma, PostgreSQL and Swagger. In this article, you will learn how to perform input validation and transformation for your API.

title: The Prisma Data Platform enables teams to collaborate using Prisma
description: The Prisma Data Platform solves problems developers face when working with databases in production, and is now generally available.

title: Build a REST API with NestJS, Prisma, PostgreSQL and Swagger
description: Learn how to build a backend REST API with NestJS, Prisma, PostgreSQL and Swagger. In this article, you will learn how to set up the project, build the API and document it with Swagger.

title: Prisma support for CockroachDB is now in GA
description: Prisma's support for CockroachDB is now in production ready! Read this article to learn about the features and benefits of Prisma with CockroachDb.

title: Announcing Prisma Day 2022 — Our First Hybrid (Berlin and online) Prisma Day!
description: We’re thrilled to announce that this summer, Prisma Day will feature both in-person and remote events and workshops. Prisma Day will take place June 15th and 16th'

title: Prisma Raises $40M to Build the Application Data Platform for Development Teams & Organizations
description: We are excited to announce that we have raised our Series B funding. Read this article to learn more about our vision of an Application Data Platform built for development teams and organizations.

description: How Prisma helps Amplication evolutionize backend development
